steps.description: Place the extruder body on a flat surface with the front face pointing upward.
steps.raw_text: Place the extruder body on a flat surface with the front face pointing upward. See Fig. 4.1 and Fig. 4.2 for correct orientation.

steps.description: Insert the Bondtech drive gear onto the motor shaft, ensuring the set screw aligns with the flat side of the shaft.
steps.raw_text: Insert the Bondtech drive gear onto the motor shaft, ensuring the set screw aligns with the flat side of the shaft. See Fig. 4.3.

steps.description: Tighten the set screw on the Bondtech drive gear using a 1.5mm hex key.
steps.raw_text: Tighten the set screw on the Bondtech drive gear using a 1.5mm hex key. Do not overtighten.

steps.description: Apply a small amount of lubricant to the lead screw threads.
steps.raw_text: Apply a small amount of lubricant to the lead screw threads. See Fig. 4.5 for the correct application area.

steps.description: Allow the lubricant to settle on the lead screw for at least 60 seconds before continuing.
steps.raw_text: Allow the lubricant to settle on the lead screw for at least 60 seconds before continuing.

steps.description: Thread the lead screw into the brass nut on the X-axis carriage from the bottom, rotating clockwise.
steps.raw_text: Thread the lead screw into the brass nut on the X-axis carriage from the bottom, rotating clockwise. Refer to Fig. 4.6 and Fig. 4.7.

steps.description: Mount the stepper motor to the extruder body using four M3x10 screws, tightening in a cross pattern.
steps.raw_text: Mount the stepper motor to the extruder body using four M3x10 screws, tightening in a cross pattern. See Fig. 4.8.

steps.description: Install the protective cap over the motor connector, pressing firmly until it clicks into place.
steps.raw_text: Install the protective cap over the motor connector, pressing firmly until it clicks into place. See Fig. 4.9.
